questions.question: What is the effect of light hitting a mirror at an angle?
questions.answer: When light hits a mirror at an angle, it reflects off the mirror and does not scatter. This is known as specular reflection.

questions.question: What type of image is formed when an object is placed between the pole and center of curvature of a concave mirror?
questions.answer: A real, inverted, and enlarged image is formed when an object is placed between the pole and center of curvature of a concave mirror.

questions.question: What is the effect of static electricity on hair?
questions.answer: Static electricity causes hair to stand up due to the transfer of electrons from one object to another, resulting in an imbalance of electric charge.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a mirror magnifier?
questions.answer: A mirror magnifier is used to produce a virtual, enlarged image of an object.

questions.question: What type of shadow is formed when an object blocks light completely?
questions.answer: An umbra is the darker inner shadow region formed when an object blocks light completely.

questions.question: What is the effect of two plane mirrors placed at an angle to each other?
questions.answer: When two plane mirrors are placed at an angle to each other, multiple images are formed due to the reflection of light.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a concave mirror in a telescope?
questions.answer: A concave mirror is used to produce a real, inverted image of an object, which is then magnified by the telescope.

questions.question: What type of light is formed when an object is placed at infinity from a concave mirror?
questions.answer: A parallel beam of light is formed when an object is placed at infinity from a concave mirror.

questions.question: What is the effect of static electricity on a balloon?
questions.answer: Static electricity causes a balloon to stick to other objects due to the transfer of electrons from one object to another.

questions.question: What type of image is formed when an object is placed between the focus and infinity from a concave mirror?
questions.answer: A virtual, enlarged image is formed when an object is placed between the focus and infinity from a concave mirror.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a convex lens in a camera?
questions.answer: A convex lens is used to produce a real, inverted image of an object, which is then recorded by the camera.

questions.question: What type of light is formed when an object is placed at the center of curvature from a concave mirror?
questions.answer: A parallel beam of light is formed when an object is placed at the center of curvature from a concave mirror.

questions.question: What is the effect of static electricity on a metal object?
questions.answer: Static electricity causes a metal object to attract other objects due to the transfer of electrons from one object to another.

questions.question: What type of image is formed when an object is placed between the pole and focus from a concave mirror?
questions.answer: A real, inverted, and enlarged image is formed when an object is placed between the pole and focus from a concave mirror.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a lens in a microscope?
questions.answer: A lens is used to produce a virtual, enlarged image of an object, which is then magnified by the microscope.
